sourceMovie.title: Skyscraper
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Skyscraper (2018) as a action theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The action and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Weekend in Taipei
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Skyscraper clicked for you, Weekend in Taipei (2024) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and thriller, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Extraction 2
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Skyscraper clicked for you, Extraction 2 (2023) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Skyscraper?
faq.answer: Try Weekend in Taipei, Extraction 2, Damsel—each shares DNA with Skyscraper while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Skyscraper?
faq.answer: Skyscraper (2018) is best described as Action, Thriller, Adventure. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Skyscraper worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.3/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this action lane.
